source: curated from wikidata canonical labels + wikipedia redirects
notes: seed tier of the canonical alias registry. high-value proper nouns that must never fork across sources. extend this file for additional coverage; the runtime tier (db/alias_registry.json) is auto-populated and should not be edited by hand.

openai.description: American artificial intelligence research organization founded in 2015, creator of the GPT family of language models and ChatGPT.

anthropic.description: American AI safety company founded in 2021 by former OpenAI researchers, creator of the Claude family of large language models.

google.description: American multinational technology company specializing in internet-related services, AI research, and consumer products.

google deepmind.description: British-American AI research laboratory owned by Google, created by merging DeepMind and Google Brain in 2023.

meta.description: American multinational technology conglomerate, parent company of Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and AI research group Meta AI (FAIR).

meta ai.description: Meta Platforms' AI research division, formerly Facebook AI Research (FAIR).

microsoft.description: American multinational technology corporation headquartered in Redmond, Washington.

microsoft research.description: Microsoft's research division focused on computer science, software engineering, and AI.

apple.description: American multinational technology company headquartered in Cupertino, California.

amazon.description: American multinational technology company focusing on e-commerce, cloud computing, and AI.

nvidia.description: American multinational corporation that designs graphics processing units and AI accelerators.

ibm.description: American multinational technology corporation, one of the oldest computing companies.

intel.description: American multinational corporation that designs and manufactures microprocessors.

amd.description: American multinational semiconductor company producing CPUs and GPUs.

tesla.description: American electric vehicle and clean energy company led by Elon Musk.

xai.description: American AI startup founded by Elon Musk in 2023, creator of the Grok family of large language models.

mistral ai.description: French AI startup founded in 2023 that develops open-weight large language models.

cohere.description: Canadian multinational technology company focused on enterprise AI and large language models.

hugging face.description: American-French company providing an open-source platform and hub for machine learning models, datasets, and libraries.

stability ai.description: AI company best known for releasing the open-weight Stable Diffusion image generation model.

runway.description: AI research company focused on generative video and creative tools.

perplexity.description: AI-powered answer engine and conversational search company founded in 2022.

palantir.description: American public software company that builds data analytics platforms for government and enterprise.

chatgpt.description: OpenAI's conversational AI product powered by the GPT family of large language models, launched in November 2022.

gpt-4.description: OpenAI's multimodal large language model released in March 2023, successor to GPT-3.5.

gpt-4o.description: OpenAI's flagship multimodal model released in 2024, designed for text, image, and audio.

gpt-3.description: OpenAI's 175-billion-parameter autoregressive language model released in 2020.

gpt-3 5.description: OpenAI's interim LLM family between GPT-3 and GPT-4, powering the initial release of ChatGPT.

claude.description: Anthropic's family of large language models and associated conversational AI assistant, first released in 2023.

claude 3.description: Anthropic's third generation of the Claude language model family, released in March 2024.

claude 3 5 sonnet.description: Anthropic's Claude 3.5 Sonnet large language model, released in June 2024.

claude sonnet 4 6.description: Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 4.6 large language model.

gemini.description: Google DeepMind's family of multimodal large language models, successor to PaLM, launched in December 2023.

llama.description: Meta's open-weight family of large language models, first released in February 2023.

mistral.description: Open-weight large language model family developed by Mistral AI.

grok.description: xAI's large language model and conversational AI product integrated with X (formerly Twitter).

gemma.description: Google's family of open-weight lightweight language models derived from Gemini research.

bert.description: Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers, a transformer-based language model released by Google in 2018.

t5.description: Google's Text-to-Text Transfer Transformer, a unified text-to-text encoder-decoder model.

bge-m3.description: Multilingual, multi-functionality, multi-granularity text embedding model from BAAI described by Chen et al. (2024).

stable diffusion.description: Open-weight latent diffusion text-to-image generation model originally released by Stability AI in 2022.

midjourney.description: Independent research lab producing a generative AI image creation service of the same name.

sora.description: OpenAI's text-to-video generative model announced in February 2024.

whisper.description: OpenAI's open-source automatic speech recognition model released in September 2022.

pytorch.description: Open-source deep learning framework originally developed by Meta AI and now governed by the PyTorch Foundation.

tensorflow.description: Open-source machine learning framework developed by Google Brain.

jax.description: Google's numerical computing library with composable function transformations (grad, jit, vmap).

keras.description: High-level neural networks API, integrated into TensorFlow and running on multiple backends.

scikit-learn.description: Open-source Python machine learning library built on NumPy, SciPy, and matplotlib.

numpy.description: Fundamental package for scientific computing in Python providing N-dimensional arrays.

pandas.description: Open-source Python library providing data structures and data analysis tools.

transformers.description: Hugging Face's Python library providing pretrained transformer models for NLP, vision, and audio tasks.

llama cpp.description: Open-source C/C++ inference runtime for LLaMA-family language models with GGUF quantization support.

ollama.description: Open-source tool for running large language models locally with a simple command-line interface.

vllm.description: High-throughput and memory-efficient inference engine for large language models using PagedAttention.

langchain.description: Open-source framework for building applications powered by large language models.

llamaindex.description: Data framework for connecting custom data sources to large language models, originally called GPT Index.

github.description: Microsoft-owned web-based hosting service for version control and collaborative software development using Git.

git.description: Distributed version control system created by Linus Torvalds in 2005.

docker.description: Platform-as-a-service product for delivering software in lightweight containers.

kubernetes.description: Open-source container orchestration system originally designed by Google.

linux.description: Family of open-source Unix-like operating systems based on the Linux kernel.

macos.description: Apple's operating system for Mac computers, derived from NeXTSTEP and Unix.

windows.description: Microsoft's family of graphical operating systems for personal computers.

python.description: High-level general-purpose programming language created by Guido van Rossum, widely used in machine learning and data science.

rust.description: Systems programming language focused on memory safety, concurrency, and performance.

typescript.description: Microsoft-developed strongly typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript.

javascript.description: High-level interpreted programming language that forms the core of web development alongside HTML and CSS.

go.description: Statically typed compiled programming language designed at Google by Robert Griesemer, Rob Pike, and Ken Thompson.

java.description: Class-based, object-oriented programming language originally developed by Sun Microsystems.

c++.description: General-purpose programming language created by Bjarne Stroustrup as an extension of C.

swift.description: General-purpose multi-paradigm compiled programming language developed by Apple.

kotlin.description: Statically typed programming language developed by JetBrains, officially supported for Android development.

sqlite.description: Self-contained, serverless, zero-configuration, transactional SQL database engine.

postgresql.description: Free and open-source object-relational database management system.

mysql.description: Open-source relational database management system owned by Oracle.

redis.description: In-memory data structure store used as a database, cache, and message broker.

elasticsearch.description: Distributed, RESTful search and analytics engine built on Apache Lucene.

aws.description: Amazon's comprehensive cloud computing platform, the largest public cloud provider.

azure.description: Microsoft's cloud computing platform providing infrastructure, platform, and software services.

gcp.description: Google's cloud computing services suite including compute, storage, databases, and ML tools.

coreweave.description: Specialized cloud provider focused on GPU compute for AI and machine learning workloads.

wikipedia.description: Free multilingual online encyclopedia written and maintained by a community of volunteer editors.

wikidata.description: Free collaborative multilingual knowledge graph hosted by the Wikimedia Foundation.

arxiv.description: Open-access preprint repository for scientific papers in physics, mathematics, computer science, and related fields.

reddit.description: American social news aggregation, content rating, and discussion platform.

twitter.description: Social networking service previously known as Twitter, rebranded to X in 2023.

youtube.description: Google-owned online video sharing and social media platform.

facebook.description: Online social media and social networking service owned by Meta.

instagram.description: Photo and video sharing social networking service owned by Meta.

whatsapp.description: Cross-platform centralized instant messaging and voice-over-IP service owned by Meta.

discord.description: Voice, video and text communication service used by over a hundred million users.

slack.description: Enterprise messaging and collaboration platform owned by Salesforce.

notion.description: Productivity and note-taking application combining documents, wikis, and project management.

obsidian.description: Personal knowledge base and note-taking application operating on local Markdown files with bidirectional linking.

vscode.description: Microsoft's free, open-source code editor built on Electron and TypeScript.

cursor.description: AI-first code editor forked from Visual Studio Code.

copilot.description: AI-powered code completion tool developed by GitHub and OpenAI, released in 2021.

claude code.description: Anthropic's agentic command-line coding tool powered by the Claude language model family.

imagenet.description: Large visual database designed for use in visual object recognition research, organized according to the WordNet hierarchy.

coco.description: Microsoft Common Objects in Context, a large-scale object detection, segmentation, and captioning dataset.

mmlu.description: Massive Multitask Language Understanding benchmark measuring LLM accuracy across 57 subjects.

glue.description: General Language Understanding Evaluation, a collection of natural language understanding tasks.

squad.description: Stanford Question Answering Dataset, a reading comprehension benchmark.

humaneval.description: OpenAI's benchmark for evaluating code generation models on hand-written Python programming problems.

wikitext.description: Language modeling dataset of tokens extracted from verified Good and Featured Wikipedia articles.

common crawl.description: Nonprofit organization that crawls the web and freely provides petabytes of data to the public.

the pile.description: 825 GiB diverse, open-source language modeling dataset constructed by EleutherAI.

acl.description: Association for Computational Linguistics, the premier international scientific and professional society for computational linguistics.

emnlp.description: Conference on Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing, a leading NLP venue.
aliases: empirical methods in natural language processing

neurips.description: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems, a premier machine learning and computational neuroscience venue.

icml.description: International Conference on Machine Learning, one of the top machine learning conferences.

iclr.description: International Conference on Learning Representations, a top venue for deep learning research.

cvpr.description: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition, the premier annual computer vision venue.

ieee.description: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers, the world's largest technical professional organization.
aliases: institute of electrical and electronics engineers

acm.description: Association for Computing Machinery, a US-based international learned society for computing.

mit.description: Massachusetts Institute of Technology, a private research university in Cambridge, Massachusetts.

stanford.description: Private research university in Stanford, California, one of the top computer science institutions.

berkeley.description: University of California, Berkeley, a public land-grant research university in Berkeley, California.

cmu.description: Private research university in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, prominent in computer science and AI.

transformer.description: Deep learning architecture based on self-attention mechanisms introduced in the 2017 paper 'Attention Is All You Need' by Vaswani et al.

attention mechanism.description: Neural network technique that enables models to dynamically focus on relevant portions of the input sequence.

backpropagation.description: Gradient-based optimization algorithm for training neural networks by propagating errors backwards through the network.

reinforcement learning.description: Machine learning paradigm where an agent learns to make decisions by interacting with an environment and receiving rewards.

rlhf.description: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback, a technique for aligning language models with human preferences.

rag.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation, a technique combining retrieval from a knowledge base with generative language modeling.

chain-of-thought.description: Prompting technique where language models are encouraged to reason step by step before producing a final answer.

fine-tuning.description: Adapting a pretrained model to a downstream task by continuing training on task-specific data.

lora.description: Low-Rank Adaptation, a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method that injects trainable low-rank matrices into pretrained model weights.

bm25.description: Probabilistic information retrieval ranking function for estimating document relevance to a query.

tf-idf.description: Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency, a numerical statistic reflecting word importance in a document within a corpus.

cosine similarity.description: Measure of similarity between two non-zero vectors defined as the cosine of the angle between them.

fts5.description: SQLite's full-text search extension providing inverted-index search with BM25 ranking.

word2vec.description: Shallow neural network model for learning word embeddings from large text corpora, introduced by Mikolov et al. at Google in 2013.

glove.description: Global Vectors for Word Representation, an unsupervised learning algorithm for obtaining word embeddings.

cnn.description: Convolutional Neural Network, a class of deep learning models particularly well-suited for image and signal processing.

rnn.description: Recurrent Neural Network, a class of artificial neural networks where connections form a temporal sequence.

lstm.description: Long Short-Term Memory, a recurrent neural network architecture designed to handle long-range dependencies.

gan.description: Generative Adversarial Network, a machine learning framework where two neural networks contest each other in a zero-sum game.

vae.description: Variational Autoencoder, a generative model that uses variational inference to learn latent representations.

diffusion model.description: Class of generative models that learn to reverse a progressive noising process applied to training data.

embedding.description: Dense vector representation of discrete objects such as words, sentences, or entities in a continuous feature space.

quantization.description: Technique for reducing the precision of neural network weights and activations to lower memory and compute requirements.

distillation.description: Process of transferring knowledge from a large teacher model to a smaller student model, introduced by Hinton et al. 2015.

pruning.description: Model compression technique that removes redundant neural network parameters without significantly harming performance.

gguf.description: GPT-Generated Unified Format, a file format for storing quantized neural network models used by llama.cpp.

onnx.description: Open Neural Network Exchange, an open format for representing machine learning models.
